mappings.rationale: Directly covers East African history including coastal trading cities, the slave trade, and European exploration discussed throughout this section.
Relevant to subsections B2 and C1 on the political development of East African kingdoms and military kingships.
Relevant to subsection B1b on the spread of Islam along the coast and indigenous religious traditions of the interior peoples.
Relevant to subsection B1b on the spread of Islam via the Shirazi dynasty and the growth of Islamic coastal towns.
Relevant to subsection C1c on European exploratory and missionary activities and British colonial interest in East Africa.
Directly relevant to subsections B1c and C1c on Portuguese invasions from 1502 and European exploratory activities.
Relevant to subsection B1c on Portuguese occupation, Omani influence, and the broader pattern of imperial activity in East Africa.
Relevant to the section's coverage of the impact of European colonial and missionary activities on indigenous East African peoples.
Relevant to subsections A and B2a on the Stone Age origins of East African peoples and archaeological evidence from the Rift Valley.
Relevant to subsection B1a-b on medieval commercial contacts with Arabia and the development of Muslim coastal trading cities.
